sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Getafe comes into this match in strong form, having won three of their last five matches, including a dominant 11-0 cup win. Mallorca, on the other hand, has struggled recently, managing just two wins in their last five games and sitting in 18th place in the league standings. Getafe's attacking momentum, driven by in-form players like Borja Mayoral and Juanmi, gives them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters between these teams, results have been split, with each side securing a win. Getafe won the most recent clash 2-1 in May 2025, showing their ability to exploit Mallorca’s defense. Historically, their matches have often been tight, with low-scoring outcomes.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Mallorca struggles offensively, ranking 15th in goals scored (11) and conceding 18 goals this season. Getafe, positioned 7th in the league, has a more balanced approach with 12 goals scored and 13 conceded, showing resilience in defense. Their superior league standing and recent form tilt the scales in their favor.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Mallorca, Vedat Muriqi (5 goals) will be crucial in breaking through Getafe’s defense. Getafe’s Luis Milla, leading the league in assists (6), and Borja Mayoral, with 5 goals in recent matches, will likely dictate the tempo. These players could be game-changers in a closely contested match.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Considering Sofascore’s prediction (51% away win) and Getafe’s current momentum, they are the favorites to win. Mallorca’s defensive vulnerabilities and poor home form could be exploited by Getafe’s attacking threats. Expect a low-scoring but competitive match with Getafe likely edging it.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half of the match between Mallorca and Getafe at Estadi de Son Moix ended with Mallorca leading 1-0 thanks to an early goal from Vedat Muriqi in the 14th minute. Mallorca capitalized on their opportunity, converting one of their limited chances into a goal. Getafe, despite dominating possession and creating more entries into the final third and penalty area, failed to find the back of the net. The away side earned numerous corner kicks but couldn’t turn their pressure into a goal. Both teams showed resilience in duels, with Mallorca slightly edging out in aerial battles.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Mallorca's performance
halftimeSections.text: Mallorca played a more pragmatic game in the first half, focusing on defending their lead after scoring early. Their defensive structure was solid, as seen in their clearances and ability to limit Getafe’s clear scoring opportunities despite being under pressure. Vedat Muriqi was the standout performer, not only scoring the crucial goal but also contributing to the team’s defensive efforts. Johan Mojica also played a key role with a vital assist, showcasing creativity on the flank. Mallorca will likely aim to maintain their compact shape in the second half while looking for counter-attacking opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Getafe's performance
halftimeSections.text: Getafe controlled much of the ball in the first half and consistently pushed forward, evidenced by their high number of penalty area entries and corner kicks. Despite their territorial dominance, they lacked the finishing touch, failing to convert any big chances. Players like Mauro Arambarri showed promise in attack, but their efforts were not enough to break through Mallorca’s defense. Discipline was also an issue, with two yellow cards picked up. Getafe will need to sharpen their attacking play and be more clinical if they are to turn the game around in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking momentum between the two sides. Getafe started with intent, pushing forward in the opening minutes. However, Mallorca quickly shifted the momentum in their favor with sustained attacking spells leading up to their goal. After conceding, Getafe regained control with several prolonged periods of pressure, particularly in the middle and latter stages of the half. Mallorca had brief spells of attack post-goal but largely focused on defending. Towards the end of the half, Getafe once again dominated the attacking intent, but Mallorca held firm.
